activation_mode.mechanism: activation_modes extension: always (unconditional), conditional (glob patterns), model (AI decides), manual (explicit toggle)

auto_memory.mechanism: auto_generated_memories: Windsurf AI generates persistent memories stored in ~/.codeium/windsurf/memories/ as rule-like files

cross_provider_recognition.mechanism: agents_md_auto_scoping: Windsurf reads both AGENTS.md and agents.md (case-insensitive) files from other providers and applies them with workspace-aware scoping

file_imports.mechanism: No @-import or file inclusion syntax documented for Windsurf rule files

hierarchical_loading.mechanism: rules_hierarchical_discovery: Windsurf discovers .windsurf/rules/ directories in the current workspace, all sub-directories, and parent directories up to the git root; rules from all levels are merged and deduplicated

providerExtensions.name: Four activation modes via trigger frontmatter
providerExtensions.summary: Rules select one of four activation modes (always_on, model_decision, glob, or manual) via frontmatter.

providerExtensions.name: AGENTS.md and agents.md: location-based auto-scoping without frontmatter
providerExtensions.summary: Both AGENTS.md and agents.md (case-insensitive) are recognized; root-level files are always-on while subdirectory files become auto-glob rules scoped to that subtree.

providerExtensions.name: Global, workspace, and system rule scopes
providerExtensions.summary: Rules load from three user scopes plus an enterprise system scope, with per-scope character limits and OS-specific managed paths.

providerExtensions.name: Auto-generated memories distinct from rules
providerExtensions.summary: Cascade auto-generates workspace-scoped memories during conversations, stored separately from user-authored Rules and not committed to git.

providerExtensions.name: Multi-level hierarchical rules discovery
providerExtensions.summary: Windsurf searches for .windsurf/rules/ directories in the current workspace, all subdirectories, and parent directories up to the git root; rules from multiple locations are merged and deduplicated, displayed with shortest relative paths.
